lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Greetings. Can I help you at all?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any quests I could do?

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: A quest? What a strange notion. Do you normally go around asking complete strangers for quests?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, yes, now you come to mention it.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Oh, ok then. Well, no, I don't; sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: A good question; straight and to the point.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: My name is Matthias, I am a falconer, and this is where I train my birds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds like fun; could I have a go?

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Training falcons is a lot of work and I doubt you're up to the task. However, I suppose I could let you try hunting with one.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: I have some tamer birds that I occasionally lend to rich noblemen who consider it a sufficiently refined sport for their tastes, and you look like the kind who might appreciate a good hunt.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: I'd have to request a small fee, mind you; how does 500 gold pieces sound?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have that much with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, that seems reasonable.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Good, however you really need both hands free. I'd suggest that you put away your weapons and gloves before we start.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Good, however you really need both hands free. I'd suggest that you take off some of that kit you're wearing before we start.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Don't worry; I'll keep an eye on you to make sure you don't upset it too much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not interested then, thanks.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Well, you're welcome to come back if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Absolutely, are you ready?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't sound like my sort of thing.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Fair enough; it does require a great deal of patience and skill, so I can understand if you might feel intimidated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's this falconry thing all about then?

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Well, some people see it as a sport, although such a term does not really convey the amount of patience and dedication required to be proficient at the task.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Putting it simply, it is the training and use of birds of prey in hunting quarry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So it's like keeping a pet then?

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Not exactly, no. Such a bird can never really be considered tame in the same way that a dog can.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: They can be trained to associate people or places with food though, and, as such, a good falconer can get a trained bird to do as he wishes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have a go with your bird?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I invest in your setup here?

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: If you'd like to put money forward for training more falcons, be my guest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would I get any benefit from this?

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: I suppose I could let you use my falcons for free if you stump up enough cash. How does 500,000 coins sound?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I don't have that much on me right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, that seems a little steep. I'll think on it.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: I appreciate that you want to invest more, but I really don't need it right now!

sections.title: If the falconer's glove is equipped

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Ah, you're back. How are you getting along with [him/her/them] then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's certainly harder than it looks.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Sorry, but I was talking to the falcon, not you. But yes it is; have you had enough yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'd like to keep trying a little longer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok then, just come talk to me when you're done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll leave it for now.

sections.title: If the player lost the falcon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, er, your falcon ran away.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Falcons aren't domesticated you know. You can't expect them to just automatically return to you. Fortunately for you, they can learn to associate areas with food. He'll probably be back on his post.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Do you want me to fetch him for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, please.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Right, try to be a bit more careful this time; these birds take a long time to raise. I'd rather not lose one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I think I'll leave it for now.

sections.title: If the falcon is holding a kebbit

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Matthias
lines.text: Hmm, well it looks like you've got the hang of setting falcons on creatures. However, I suggest you keep track of it; left to its own devices it'll be more than happy to fly off on its own to find food.
